1.title: 📖 Fundamentals
1.description: Learn the basics of prompt crafting: clarity, goals, audience, and context.
1.body:
Prompt Engineering is the art of crafting instructions for AI models to get the best possible results.

🎯 Key Elements of a Good Prompt:

  • Clarity: Use direct and specific language
  • Goal: Clearly state what you want
  • Audience: Who will read or use the output?
  • Context: Provide sufficient background on the topic
✏️ Example:
❌ Weak: "Write something about technology"
✅ Strong: "Write a 200-word article about AI in medicine, aimed at medical students, in simple English"

💡 Golden Tip:

The more details you provide, the fewer guesses the model makes, and the more accurate the result.

2.title: 📝 Adding Details
2.description: Learn how to control length, tone, format, and constraints in your prompts.
2.body:
After mastering the basics, it's time to add precise details that make your prompts professional.

🎛️ Key Control Elements:

  • Length: Specify the number of words or pages
  • Tone: Formal, friendly, motivational, technical, etc.
  • Format: Bullet points, paragraphs, tables, numbered lists
  • Constraints: Explicitly tell the model what to avoid
✏️ Advanced Example:
"Write a marketing plan for a new product, 300 words long, with a motivational tone, in bullet points, and do not mention competitors by name."

3.title: ⚡ Advanced Techniques
3.description: Learn CoT, Few-shot, and task decomposition techniques for smarter responses.
3.body:
🧠 1. Chain of Thought (CoT):

Ask the model to outline its reasoning steps before answering.

"Let's think step by step: First... Second... Third... Now the final answer is..."

📚 2. Few-shot Learning:

Provide 2-3 examples of inputs and outputs you want.

"Example 1: Input X → Output Y
Example 2: Input A → Output B
Now: Input Z → ?"

🧩 3. Task Decomposition:

Break down complex tasks into smaller steps.

"I want you to: 1. Analyze the audience 2. Choose channels 3. Write the message 4. Summarize everything"

4.title: 🔄 Interactive & Iterative
4.description: Learn to build step-by-step dialogues, control creativity, and use multiple roles.
4.body:
💬 1. Step-by-step Dialogue:

Don't ask for everything at once; build the response step by step.

🎨 2. Controlling Creativity (Temperature):

  • 0.0: Precise and predictable (suitable for facts)
  • 0.7: Balanced (default)
  • 1.0: Creative and surprising (suitable for new ideas)

🎭 3. Multiple Roles:

"You now combine 3 expertise levels: Marketing Expert, Data Analyst, and Content Writer. I want an analysis from each perspective."

🚫 4. Negative Prompts:

"Write an article about technology, but: do not mention company names, do not use complex terms, and do not exceed 150 words."

5.title: 🏆 Professional & Building
5.description: Learn to build personal templates, self-evaluation, security, and SKILL.md files.
5.body:
📋 1. Personal Templates:

Create a fixed template you use for any task, then fill in the blanks as needed.

🔍 2. Self-Evaluation of Outputs:

Ask the model to evaluate its own response before presenting it.

"After you write the post, evaluate it according to: Does it achieve the goal? Is the tone appropriate? How can it be improved?"

🔒 3. Prompt Security:

Protect your prompts from injection attacks and avoid placing sensitive information in prompts.

📄 4. SKILL.md Files:

Package your skills into structured files that any model can read and execute automatically.
